content:
{
  "title": "Symbolic Chain-of-Thought Prompting for Enhanced Mathematical Reasoning in LLMs",
  "problem": "Large language models often falter on complex mathematical problems due to ambiguity in reasoning steps and insufficient symbolic manipulation. The challenge is to devise a prompting method that consistently guides LLMs toward more accurate, stepwise mathematical solutions.",
  "motivation": "Improving LLM mathematical reasoning is crucial for applications in education, science, and engineering, where correctness and clarity matter. Recent advances in LLMs have not fully closed the gap in symbolic math reasoning, despite progress in Chain-of-Thought (CoT) prompting. A method that integrates explicit symbolic cues could further enhance reliability and interpretability.",
  "method": "We propose Symbolic Chain-of-Thought (S-CoT) prompting, where natural language problem statements are augmented with explicit symbolic representations and type-annotated variables at each reasoning step. Prompts are constructed to alternate between NL explanation and LaTeX/MathML-formatted math expressions, enforcing variable tracking and type propagation (e.g., 'Let x ∈ ℝ'). The method includes system-level instructions to explicitly verify each derived equation before proceeding. This hybrid sequence constrains the model to produce valid symbolic steps, reducing hallucinations and semantic drift.",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate S-CoT on GSM8K and MATH datasets, comparing against standard Chain-of-Thought and Program-of-Thought baselines using GPT-4. Metrics include exact match accuracy and stepwise consistency (symbolic validity at each step). Ablations will isolate the effect of symbolic annotations and alternating format. Statistical significance will be assessed via bootstrap resampling."
}
